meta.title: URI Faculty Development Training Calendar: Available PD from URI's Member Organizations
meta.subtitle: Available professional development from URI's member organizations
notes: The three starter DOCX files were imported locally on June 2, 2026.
The internal training grid labeled DO NOT USE was used only for planning signals such as audience, timing, and topic gaps; its internal presenter and logistics details were not published.
NCFDD, CIRTL, and CUR access were verified from URI or user-provided membership documentation. EAB and AAC&U access notes still need local confirmation for member-only or paid items.

documentImports.title: Faculty Membership Resources.docx
documentImports.use: Added NCFDD and CIRTL member resources and self-paced faculty development options.

documentImports.title: Assoc Dean, Chairs and New Faculty Training Grid DO NOT USE.docx
documentImports.use: Used only to improve yearlong timing and audience alignment.

documentImports.title: System for sharing key CUR and other UR resources.docx
documentImports.use: Verified URI CUR Enhanced Institutional Membership and added CUR activation, community, publication, and undergraduate research resources.

removed.title: An Equity-Minded Approach to Faculty Tenure and Promotion

removed: ConnectUR 2026 Onsite-Only or Bundle Registration Deadline

accessNotes.summary: URI states that it is an NCFDD institutional member and that all URI faculty, postdoctoral scholars, and graduate students can use NCFDD resources.

URI ATL points faculty/future faculty to CIRTL. CIRTL courses and workshops prioritize participants from member institutions; CIRTL policy states there are no network fees for participants from network institutions.
accessNotes.action: Create a CIRTL account and register through the event page.

accessNotes.summary: EAB resources and webinars use public forms and partner-login paths, but no public URI page documenting faculty access was found.
accessNotes.action: Confirm the URI partner-login route with the Provost/ATL or the office that manages EAB access.

accessNotes.summary: URI has an Institutional - Enhanced membership in CUR, allowing any URI faculty member, mentor, administrator, student, or staff member to join CUR at no additional individual cost.
accessNotes.action: Activate enhanced membership benefits through CUR, then use CUR Community, publications, divisions, and member resources.

accessNotes.summary: AAC&U AI Week webinars were listed publicly as free; AAC&U institutes and conferences are often paid.
accessNotes.action: Prioritize public/free webinars and recordings. Screen all institutes and conferences for cost.

trainings.title: Beyond Efficiency: Reimagining AI's Purpose in Teaching, Learning, and Research
trainings.access: Free/no network fee; CIRTL member-institution participants receive priority when seats are limited.
trainings.description: A practical AI session focused on deeper learning, creativity, human-centered innovation, critical thinking, collaboration, and reflective practice.
trainings.whyInclude: Strong first summer AI session for faculty who want to move beyond efficiency toward thoughtful course and research design.

trainings.title: UDL + AI Design Studio
trainings.access: Free/no network fee for network participants; capped at 15.
trainings.description: Participants redesign one real teaching artifact using Universal Design for Learning and AI as a design assistant.
trainings.whyInclude: Applied, artifact-producing course that can support course revision and teaching evidence for review files.

trainings.title: 14-Day Writing Challenge: Summer Session
trainings.access: Free; URI NCFDD membership verified.
trainings.description: A structured two-week challenge in which participants commit to at least 30 minutes of writing each weekday and log progress in an online community.
trainings.whyInclude: Creates summer writing momentum before fall preparation begins.

trainings.title: Critical AI Literacy for Teachers and Students: Prompting, Evaluating, and Ethically Using Generative AI
trainings.access: Listed as free; capped at 60.
trainings.description: Explores AI prompt literacy, critical evaluation of AI outputs, and ethical disclosure practices across disciplines.
trainings.whyInclude: Practical, faculty-facing AI literacy that can inform course policies and student guidance.

trainings.title: Writing and Writing Instruction in the Era of AI
trainings.dateLabel: Recording; original AAC&U AI Week ran April 27-30, 2026
trainings.access: AI Week was listed as free and open; recordings shared with registrants.
trainings.description: A webinar for faculty who teach writing or use writing for learning and assessment in an AI-disrupted context.
trainings.whyInclude: Directly connects two priority themes: writing pedagogy and generative AI.

trainings.title: 14-Day Writing Challenge: Back-to-School Session
trainings.access: Free; URI NCFDD membership verified.
trainings.description: A structured two-week challenge to rebuild writing routines before the academic year begins.
trainings.whyInclude: Well-timed pre-semester writing reset.

trainings.title: Construction Kit for Faculty AI Resources
trainings.dateLabel: Self-paced; recommended for August course planning
trainings.access: Confirm URI EAB partner access before listing as a member benefit.
trainings.description: Toolkit for building faculty AI policy guidance and resource lists for curricular and teaching practice.
trainings.whyInclude: Useful for departments that need a practical AI resource list before fall syllabi are finalized.

trainings.title: How to Develop a Daily Writing Practice
trainings.access: NCFDD member resource; URI NCFDD membership verified.
trainings.description: Writing routine webinar that explicitly connects daily writing practice to tenure and promotion expectations.
trainings.whyInclude: Good first-month-of-fall intervention for sustainable scholarly productivity.

trainings.title: 14-Day Writing Challenge: Fall Session
trainings.access: Free; URI NCFDD membership verified.
trainings.description: A structured writing challenge after fall teaching routines have started to settle.
trainings.whyInclude: A timely fall writing push for manuscripts, grant proposals, books, exhibits, and other scholarly work.

trainings.title: Mastering Academic Time Management
trainings.access: NCFDD member resource; URI NCFDD membership verified.
trainings.description: Time management, academic writing, support networks, and alignment with institutional and personal priorities.
trainings.whyInclude: Good fit for new and early-career faculty navigating writing expectations and first-year overload.

trainings.title: The AIs Go Marching On: Finding Our Way with AI in Education
trainings.access: NCFDD recording; URI NCFDD membership verified.
trainings.description: Practical and ethical approaches to AI in teaching and learning, including hands-on activities and draft materials.
trainings.whyInclude: Works well as a fall teaching-and-learning discussion session after faculty have seen AI issues in live courses.

trainings.title: Higher Education AI Resource Center
trainings.access: Confirm URI EAB partner access before listing member-only assets.
trainings.description: Research, strategy, and guidance on AI across student success, enrollment, and higher education strategy.
trainings.whyInclude: Useful for chairs, deans, and faculty leaders who need governance and institutional-strategy context.

trainings.title: Author Insights: Characteristics of Excellence in Undergraduate Research (COEUR) 2.0
trainings.access: Available through URI's CUR Enhanced Institutional Membership after activation; screen any separately priced event or course.
trainings.description: COEUR 2.0 offers a framework for building successful and accessible undergraduate research programs.
trainings.whyInclude: Helps faculty and programs document undergraduate research mentoring as teaching, scholarship, and service evidence.

trainings.title: CUR Mentoring Webinar Archive
trainings.access: Available through URI's CUR Enhanced Institutional Membership after activation; screen any separately priced event or course.
trainings.description: Includes mentoring resources such as Mentoring through Failure and Mentoring in Engineering Undergraduate Research.
trainings.whyInclude: Can support faculty in documenting undergraduate mentoring quality and outcomes in annual review and promotion materials.

trainings.title: NCFDD Core Curriculum and Guest Expert Webinars
trainings.dateLabel: Monthly live/member webinars; recommended refresh in January 2027
trainings.access: NCFDD member resources; URI NCFDD membership verified.
trainings.description: NCFDD core and guest expert webinars, member library, accountability buddy matches, and writing resources.
trainings.whyInclude: A flexible January restart for faculty planning, writing, boundaries, mentoring networks, and productivity.

trainings.title: Teacher Research Thinking and Reasoning AI
trainings.access: Available through URI's CUR Enhanced Institutional Membership after activation; screen any separately priced event or course.
trainings.description: Webinar on teacher research thinking and reasoning AI, including benefits and risks.
trainings.whyInclude: Good AI option for faculty thinking about inquiry, student research, and education contexts.

trainings.title: AI at the Tipping Point: What Higher Ed Leaders Must Decide Now
trainings.access: Confirm URI EAB partner access before listing as a member benefit.
trainings.description: Higher education AI strategy webinar for leaders setting coordinated AI direction.
trainings.whyInclude: Better suited for chairs, program directors, and faculty governance than individual classroom tactics.

trainings.title: How Academic Departments Make or Break Institutional Strategy
trainings.access: Confirm URI EAB partner access before listing as a member benefit.
trainings.description: Academic program review and department-level decision-making, including decisions related to faculty hiring, course scheduling, and promotion/tenure.
trainings.whyInclude: Useful for department leaders connecting faculty work, strategy, and evaluation structures.

trainings.title: Refresh CIRTL Spring/Summer Programming
trainings.dateLabel: Refresh in April 2027 when spring/summer schedules are posted
trainings.access: CIRTL member-institution participant route verified.
trainings.description: Placeholder to refresh the page with the newest CIRTL AI, inclusive teaching, and teaching-as-research offerings.
trainings.whyInclude: CIRTL schedules change by semester and should be refreshed rather than frozen.

trainings.title: AAC&U AI Week Recordings or Next AI Week Offering
trainings.access: Use public/free webinars first; screen cost.
trainings.description: AAC&U's 2026 AI Week included free webinars on AI strategy, campus implementation, writing instruction, and leadership.
trainings.whyInclude: AI Week aligns well with the page's AI and writing focus, but future dates need refresh.

trainings.title: Year-End Dossier Sprint: Writing Plan, AI Statement, and Evidence Checklist
trainings.access: Local URI event to be created.
trainings.description: A synthesis session using NCFDD writing tools, CUR mentoring evidence, and EAB/AAC&U AI strategy resources.
trainings.whyInclude: Faculty leave with a summer writing plan, revised AI statement or course policy, and a promotion/annual-review evidence checklist.

trainings.title: Faculty Success Program
trainings.access: Do not advertise as free unless URI sponsors seats.
trainings.description: Structured, coached productivity and writing program.
trainings.whyInclude: Valuable but not part of the free/basic institutional membership list.

trainings.title: Teaching Toolkit in the Age of AI
trainings.access: NCFDD lists the 2026 course at $495; do not advertise as free unless URI purchases/sponsors access.
trainings.description: Course on assessment, grading, teaching workload, and AI-informed teaching decisions.
trainings.whyInclude: Strong topic fit, but it should be held unless funding is available.

trainings.title: ConnectUR 2026
trainings.dateLabel: Online June 17-18, 2026; in College Park June 22-24, 2026
trainings.access: URI CUR membership is verified, but event registration cost should be screened before listing as free.
trainings.description: CUR's annual conference focuses on practical strategies, leadership insights, inclusive approaches, emerging trends, scalable practices, and campus impact in undergraduate research.
trainings.whyInclude: Useful, but should not appear in a free/membership list unless covered or confirmed free.

trainings.title: Every Summer Needs a Plan
trainings.access: Automatically detected from a source page. Review access and cost.
trainings.description: ductivity Faculty Retention Faculty Development Grant Funding Institutional Success Offerings Programs Faculty Success Program Department Chair Success Program Post-Tenure Pathfinders Courses Building Your Publishing Pipeline Teaching Toolkit in the Age of AI 14-Day Writing Challenge Rethinking Your Research Funding Course Workshops Campus Workshops Rethinki
trainings.whyInclude: Newly detected by the updater because the source page matched the calendar's priority topics.

trainings.title: NCFDD Mentor Map
trainings.dateLabel: Self-paced; recommended for August mentoring and onboarding
trainings.access: NCFDD member resource; URI NCFDD membership verified.
trainings.description: A guided tool for mapping current mentoring networks, identifying unmet needs, and planning how to expand support.
trainings.whyInclude: Strong August/onboarding resource because it helps faculty build mentoring networks before annual review and promotion pressures accumulate.

trainings.title: NCFDD On-Demand Workshop Library
trainings.dateLabel: Self-paced; use throughout the academic year
trainings.access: NCFDD member resource; URI NCFDD membership verified.
trainings.description: The member library includes archived webinars, Q&A sessions, and multi-week course materials. Faculty can filter by categories such as career transitions, teaching, grant and funding, work-life balance, and wellness.
trainings.whyInclude: Best flexible option for faculty who cannot attend live workshops and need just-in-time support.

trainings.title: NCFDD Monday Motivator
trainings.access: Free sign-up; pairs well with URI's NCFDD membership.
trainings.description: A weekly productivity tip that reinforces NCFDD core-curriculum ideas in a small, sustained format.
trainings.whyInclude: Low-lift way to keep writing and productivity visible across the year.

trainings.title: NCFDD Early-Career Faculty Guidebook
trainings.dateLabel: Self-paced guidebook; recommended for new faculty orientation
trainings.access: URI-supported NCFDD guidebook from the faculty membership resources document.
trainings.description: A curated pathway of resources, webinars, and advice for the first years of faculty life.
trainings.whyInclude: Useful companion to orientation, mentoring, and first-year review conversations.

trainings.title: NCFDD Tenure and Mid-Career Faculty Guidebook
trainings.dateLabel: Self-paced guidebook; recommended for September P&T planning
trainings.access: URI-supported NCFDD guidebook from the faculty membership resources document.
trainings.description: Resources, webinars, and advice to help faculty navigate tenure, mid-career transitions, and career-path decisions.
trainings.whyInclude: Directly supports promotion/tenure readiness and mid-career planning.

trainings.title: CIRTL Instructor Materials Library
trainings.dateLabel: Self-paced; recommended for fall teaching refresh
trainings.access: Public CIRTL resource; URI CIRTL access verified.
trainings.description: Materials from selected CIRTL courses and workshops, including syllabi, course plans, handouts, slides, and assignments.
trainings.whyInclude: Useful for faculty adapting evidence-based teaching materials without waiting for a live course.

trainings.title: CIRTL Teaching-as-Research Pathway
trainings.dateLabel: Self-paced; recommended for November scholarly teaching/P&T evidence
trainings.access: Public CIRTL resource; URI CIRTL access verified.
trainings.description: Teaching-as-Research uses deliberate, systematic, and reflective inquiry to improve teaching practices and student learning outcomes.
trainings.whyInclude: Helps faculty connect teaching improvement to evidence that can be documented in annual review or promotion materials.

trainings.title: CIRTL Disciplinary Learning Communities Curricula
trainings.dateLabel: Self-paced facilitation resource; recommended for spring department learning communities
trainings.access: Public CIRTL resource; URI CIRTL access verified.
trainings.description: Two curricula and survey tools that departments can use to facilitate disciplinary learning communities around teaching and discipline-based education research.
trainings.whyInclude: Gives chairs and programs a ready structure for department-level faculty development.

trainings.title: Activate URI's CUR Enhanced Institutional Membership
trainings.access: URI has CUR Institutional - Enhanced membership; individual CUR membership is available at no additional cost to eligible URI community members.
trainings.description: Faculty, mentors, administrators, students, and staff can activate CUR membership benefits and access CUR communities, divisions, resources, and member materials.
trainings.whyInclude: This should be the first CUR item promoted so faculty can unlock the rest of the CUR resources.

trainings.title: CUR Community Online Forums
trainings.access: Available through URI's CUR Enhanced Institutional Membership after activation.
trainings.description: CUR offers online forums for disciplinary divisions, jobs, student journals, and resource-sharing among undergraduate research mentors and program leaders.
trainings.whyInclude: A lightweight way for faculty to find discipline-specific undergraduate research mentoring examples and colleagues.

trainings.title: CUR Publications and Member Resource Library
trainings.dateLabel: Self-paced; recommended for October undergraduate research mentoring
trainings.access: Many digital versions are free to CUR members; URI Enhanced Institutional Membership verified.
trainings.description: CUR publications and digital resources support undergraduate research mentoring, program development, and assessment.
trainings.whyInclude: Good source for building local mentoring guidance and documentation examples.

trainings.title: CUR Grant Dialogues
trainings.access: CUR membership verified, but event cost should be screened before listing as free.
trainings.description: A CUR and NGMA professional development event on navigating the funding landscape.
trainings.whyInclude: Strong writing/grants fit, but should not be advertised as free without cost confirmation.

trainings.title: CUR/HigherEd+ On-Demand Courses: AI, Grant Writing, and Mentoring
trainings.access: The downloaded CUR resource document notes that some HigherEd+ bundles are for a fee; screen before listing as free.
trainings.description: Potentially relevant courses include Ethical Use of AI in Higher Education, Introduction to ChatGPT for Academic Research, Master Class on Grant Writing Best Practices, and Building Mentorship and Student Supervision Skills for University Faculty.
trainings.whyInclude: Excellent topic fit for AI, writing, and mentoring, but cost status needs review.

trainings.title: URI Undergraduate Research Welcome Packet and CUR Activation Session
trainings.dateLabel: Recommended local session for new faculty orientation
trainings.access: Local URI session to be created using CUR activation instructions and undergraduate research resources.
trainings.description: A short orientation module that explains URI's CUR membership, how to activate benefits, how undergraduate research supports faculty work, and how to document undergraduate research mentoring for annual review and promotion.
trainings.whyInclude: The CUR resource-system document specifically recommends a new-faculty welcome packet, activation instructions, inspiring UR examples, and guidance on noting UR activity for P&T.

trainings.access: Automatically detected from a source page. Review access and cost before advertising.
